response:
Emily divides the 24 residents into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups. Each group receives 3 initial writing prompts, so the total number of initial prompts needed is 4 groups × 3 prompts = 12 prompts. 

Emily also prepares an additional set of 10 prompts to be shared equally among the 4 groups. Dividing 10 prompts among 4 groups gives each group 2.5 prompts, but since prompts are discrete items, Emily will need to distribute them as whole numbers. To ensure equal sharing, she will have to round up and prepare 12 extra prompts in total (3 prompts per group for 4 groups). This makes the total number of extra prompts 12. 

Therefore, the total number of prompts Emily needs to prepare is 12 (initial) + 12 (extra) = 24 prompts. The library of prompts will be well-organized, ensuring each group has access to a variety of ideas. The library will also serve as a valuable resource for future sessions. The END.
